name: Castañar de Ibor from Guadalupe – circular through Real de Guadalupe – Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara
name_original: Castañar de Ibor desde Guadalupe – circular por Real de Guadalupe – Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara
region_intro: Touring cycling around Guadalupe offers routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and the geological formations of the Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara. The region features a mix of open countryside and forested areas, with elevations that provide varied cycling experiences. Many routes traverse historical areas and offer views of the local architecture and natural features. The terrain includes both paved roads and some unpaved sections, catering to different touring cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Guadalupe The most popular touring cycling route is Castañar de Ibor from Guadalupe – circular through Real de Guadalupe – Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara, a 41.6 miles (67.0 km) trail that takes about 6 hours to complete. This route explores the Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara, offering a challenging ride through diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pico Villuercas Summit – Humilladero Pass loop from Ermita del Humilladero, a difficult 13.7 miles (22.1 km) path. This route includes significant climbs towards Pico Villuercas Summit, providing panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Guadalupe – Guadalupe Viaduct loop from Alía, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of Guadalupe and the impressive Guadalupe Viaduct, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Guadalupe is defined by its geological park features, river gorges, and challenging ascents, with routes available for experienced cyclists seeking demanding rides. The network includes 1 easy, 2 moderate, and 18 difficult routes. The routes in Guadalupe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Guadalupe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Guadalupe?
A: Guadalupe offers a diverse network of over 20 touring cycling routes. These routes traverse rolling hills, river valleys, and the unique geological formations of the Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara, providing varied experiences for cyclists.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: The touring cycling routes in Guadalupe are predominantly challenging, with 18 routes classified as difficult. There are also 2 moderate routes and 1 easy route, catering to a range of experience levels, though the region is best suited for those seeking demanding rides with significant climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: Yes, many routes in Guadalupe are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120565698/castanar-de-ibor-from-guadalupe-circular-through-real-de-guadalupe-geoparque-villuercas-ibores-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castañar de Ibor from Guadalupe – circular through Real de Guadalupe – Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option exploring the geopark.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Guadalupe's touring cycling routes?
A: You can expect a mix of paved roads and some unpaved sections, with terrain characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and the distinctive geological formations of the Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara. Routes often feature significant climbs, especially towards summits like Pico Villuercas, and offer views of open countryside and forested areas.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: While most routes in Guadalupe are geared towards experienced cyclists due to their difficulty, there is at least one easy route available. For families, it's advisable to check the specific route details for distance, elevation, and terrain to ensure it matches your family's cycling ability.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along touring cycling routes in Guadalupe?
A: Guadalupe's routes often pass through or near impressive natural features. You might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4461596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chiquita Cave&lt;/a&gt;, the panoramic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3406495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Villuercas Summit&lt;/a&gt;, or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4253512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint at the Estrecho de Peña Amarilla&lt;/a&gt;. The region is rich in natural monuments and geological formations.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling in Guadalupe?
A: Yes, the region around Guadalupe is steeped in history. Cyclists can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5717374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of Santa María de Guadalupe&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, or marvel at engineering feats like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3847287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalupe Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes traverse historical areas, offering glimpses of local architecture and heritage.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Guadalupe?
A: The touring cycling routes in Guadalupe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. Over 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging ascents and the stunning views of the geological park features and river gorges.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Guadalupe?
A: Absolutely. Guadalupe is particularly well-suited for experienced cyclists seeking demanding rides. The majority of routes, 18 out of 21, are classified as difficult. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Villuercas Summit – Humilladero Pass loop from Ermita del Humilladero&lt;/a&gt; feature significant climbs and provide panoramic views, perfect for those looking for a rigorous challenge.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes and pubs are not listed for every route, Guadalupe and its surrounding villages offer local establishments where you can refuel. It's always a good idea to plan your route and check for amenities in towns like Guadalupe or Cañamero, which are often starting or passing points for tours such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Cañamero – Cañamero loop from Cañamero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is it possible to spot wildlife while touring cycling in Guadalupe?
A: Given that Guadalupe is part of the Geoparque Villuercas-Ibores-Jara, an area known for its natural beauty and biodiversity, there is a good chance of spotting local wildlife. The region's mix of open countryside and forested areas provides habitats for various species, especially in quieter sections of the routes.

Q: What about routes for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: For beginners or those seeking a less strenuous ride, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available in Guadalupe. While the region is known for its challenging terrain, it's recommended to consult the route details on komoot to find the most suitable option, focusing on routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances.
tips:
The Royal Monastery of Santa María de Guadalupe is a 14th-century monastery located in the Spanish town of Guadalupe, in the province of Cáceres. It was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1993. Inside, you can appreciate Gothic, Mudéjar, Renaissance, Baroque, and Neoclassical architectural styles.

A place with a rich history and breathtaking architectural beauty! 🌟🏰

This site is a fantastic prelude before entering Guadeloupe. From the top you can see the contrast between the monastery and the town center with the modern viaduct in the background and the entire valley. I imagine the thoughts of the pilgrims who arrived here before there were roads and I realize that, despite the difference in cultures, we are still the same humans.
Incredible high mountain route along the Viejas River in the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark. The views are spectacular.
Castañar de Ibor is a small town in the Los Ibores region. Originally founded at the end of the 15th century along the old cattle tracks.
An incredible marvel of engineering is the Guadalupe railway viaduct over the Guadalupe Valley connecting Badajoz, Madrid and Huelva.
